com.pax.poslink.fullIntegration

Class InputAccount.InputAccountRequest

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.pax.poslink.base.BaseRequest<ManageRequest>
      • com.pax.poslink.fullIntegration.InputAccount.InputAccountRequest
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable
    Enclosing class:
    InputAccount

    public static class InputAccount.InputAccountRequest
extends BaseRequest<ManageRequest>
    The request data for input account command.
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      void setCashBackAmt(java.lang.String cashBackAmt)
      The cash back amount, $$$$$$$CC, if the field is NULL, and the switch on terminal turn on, terminal will prompt dialog to enter it.

      Flag indicates whether expiry date needs to be prompted during manual entry.

      Whether fallback to manual is allowed after reading swipe card failed.

      Not supported for now.

      Slot index of the key used to calculate PINBLOCK.

      Not supported for now.

      Flag indicates whether manual entry is allowed.

    • Method Detail

      • setManualEntryFlag

        public void setManualEntryFlag(java.lang.String manualEntryFlag)
        Flag indicates whether manual entry is allowed.
        Parameters:
        manualEntryFlag - Default as 0. Valid values: 0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.

      • setMagneticSwipeEntryFlag

        public void setMagneticSwipeEntryFlag(java.lang.String magneticSwipeEntryFlag)
        Flag indicates whether magnetic card swipe is allowed.
        Parameters:
        magneticSwipeEntryFlag - Default as 0. Valid values:
        0 - Not allowed,
        1 - Internal Pinpad,
        2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
        3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error)

      • setContactEMVEntryFlag

        public void setContactEMVEntryFlag(java.lang.String contactEMVEntryFlag)
        Flag indicates whether contact EMV entry is allowed.
        Parameters:
        contactEMVEntryFlag - Default as 0. Valid values:
        0 - Not allowed,
        1 - Internal Pinpad,
        2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
        3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error)

      • setContactlessEntryFlag

        public void setContactlessEntryFlag(java.lang.String contactlessEntryFlag)
        Flag indicates whether contactless entry is allowed.
        Parameters:
        contactlessEntryFlag - Default as 0. Valid values:
        0 - Not allowed,
        1 - Internal Pinpad,
        2 - External Pinpad (A80 only, if use other device will return param error).
        3 - External Pinpad first. If external pinpad is present, use external pinpad. If not, use internal pinpad. (A80 only, if use other device will return param error)

      • setFallbackSwipeEntryFlag

        public void setFallbackSwipeEntryFlag(java.lang.String fallbackSwipeEntryFlag)
        Whether fallback swipe is allowed after reading EMV card failed. Default as 0.
        Parameters:
        fallbackSwipeEntryFlag - 0 for not allowed. 1 for allowed.

      • setEncryptionFlag

        public void setEncryptionFlag(java.lang.String encryptionFlag)
        Data Encryption flag. Default as 0.
        0: not encrypted (default)
        1: DUKPT Triple DES Key with CBC mode
        2: DUKPT Triple DES Key with ECB mode
        3: DES/TDES method (Depends on the Key length: TDES for 24 bytes key, DES for 8/16 bytes key).
        4: Voltage E2EE.
        5: AES-128 CBC DUKPT
        6: AES-128 ECB DUKPT
        7: AES-192 CBC DUKPT
        8: AES-192 ECB DUKPT
        9: AES-256 CBC DUKPT
        10: AES-256 ECB DUKPT

        The application will convert it from hexadecimal binary array into an ASCII string. For example, the 3 bytes binary array 0x1a 0x23 0xd4 can be converted into "1a23d4".

        Parameters:
        encryptionFlag - Data Encryption flag

        public void setLaserScannerFlag(java.lang.String laserScannerFlag)
        Not supported for now.
        Parameters:
        laserScannerFlag - Default as 0. Valid values: 0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.

      • setFrontCameraFlag

        public void setFrontCameraFlag(java.lang.String frontCameraFlag)
        Not supported for now.
        Parameters:
        frontCameraFlag - Default as 0. Valid values: 0 - Not allowed, 1 - Allowed.

        public void setExpiryDatePrompt(java.lang.String expiryDatePrompt)
        Flag indicates whether expiry date needs to be prompted during manual entry.
        Parameters:
        expiryDatePrompt - Default as 0.
        Valid values:
        0: Do not prompt.
        1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
        2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed.

        Timeout Should be [200, 9999]. Or -1 for infinite timeout.

        public void setTimeOut(java.lang.String timeOut)
        Timeout Should be [200, 9999]. Or -1 for infinite timeout.
        Parameters:
        timeOut - timeout in 0.1s

      • setContinuousScreen

        public void setContinuousScreen(java.lang.String continuousScreen)
        ‘0’ default.
        ‘1’ after command is executed display doesn't go back to idle screen.
        Parameters:
        continuousScreen - Attribute: n1

      • setFallbackInsertEntryFlag

        public void setFallbackInsertEntryFlag(java.lang.String fallbackInsertEntryFlag)
        Whether fallback insert is allowed after reading contactless card failed. Default as 0.
        0 for not allowed.
        1 for allowed.
        Parameters:
        fallbackInsertEntryFlag - Attribute: n1
        Since:
        V1.03.00

      • setCustomData

        public void setCustomData(java.util.List<java.lang.String> customData)

        Custom data string to be encrypted, Used to define any special formats for sensitive data (PAN, Track2, etc).


        Custom data must be in hexadecimal format. Placeholder strings should not be converted to hex.


        Placeholder format:
        ${PARAMETER NAME}
        ${VARIABLE NAME, FORMAT,PADLEN}


        PARAMETER NAME – the data which needs encryption. Optional values:
        TRACK1DATA, TRACK2DATA, TRACK3DATA


        VARIABLE NAME – Variable name, optional values:
        TRACK1LEN, TRACK2LEN, TRACK3LEN


        FORMAT – The format of the data, optional values: HEX.


        PADLEN – The number of padding bytes.


        Ex1:
        Original String = “pq${TRACK2DATA}abc”
        Convert custom string to hex: “7071${TRACK2DATA}616263”


        Ex2: Multiple Strings
        [“7071${TRACK2DATA}", "${TRACK1LEN, HEX, 4}616263”]


        Attribute: Var

        Since:
        V1.09.01

      • setFallbackToManualEntryFlag

        public void setFallbackToManualEntryFlag(java.lang.String fallbackToManualEntryFlag)
        Whether fallback to manual is allowed after reading swipe card failed. Magnetic Swipe Entry Flag must be enabled.

        Default as 0.
        0 for not allowed.
        1 for allowed.

        Attribute: n1

        Only valid when TransType = INPUTACCOUNTWITHEMV
        Since:
        V1.12.00

      • setKeySLot

        public void setKeySLot(java.lang.String keySLot)
        Slot index of the key used to calculate PINBLOCK. start from 1. Default to 1
        Data Encryption key slot number, start from 1.
        For setEncryptionFlag(java.lang.String) as 1, 2, 5, 6
        Pax Platform Pax Models Valid value
        MonitorS80,S300,S500,SP30,D210. 1-40(DES DUKPT and Master Session)
        Prolin D200,D220, S920,Q20,Q30,Px5,Px7. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)
        PayDroid A60, A80, A920, A930, Aries Series. 1-99 (DES DUKPT and Master Session) 1-40(AES DUKPT)
        For setEncryptionFlag(java.lang.String) as 3, valid value is 2-89
        Parameters:
        keySLot - Data Encrytion key slot number, start from 1.

      • setMinAccountLength

        public void setMinAccountLength(int minAccountLength)
        MIN length for account. Default as 10. Valid value [1, 32].
        Parameters:
        minAccountLength - MIN length for account

      • setMinAccountLength

        public void setMinAccountLength(java.lang.String minAccountLength)
        MIN length for account. Default as 10. Valid value [1, 32].
        Parameters:
        minAccountLength - MIN length for account

      • setMaxAccountLength

        public void setMaxAccountLength(java.lang.String maxAccountLength)

      • setCVVPrompt

        public void setCVVPrompt(java.lang.String cvvPrompt)
        Whether CVV need to be prompted during manual entry, Default as 0;
        Parameters:
        cvvPrompt - 0: Do not prompt.
        1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
        2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed.

      • setZipPrompt

        public void setZipPrompt(java.lang.String zipPrompt)
        Whether Zip code need to prompted during manual entry

        Default as 0:
        0: Do not prompt.
        1: Prompt: Bypass Allowed
        2: Prompt: Bypass Not Allowed.

        Parameters:
        zipPrompt - The zip code, only CREDIT supports it. the min length is 5 character.

      • setTagList

        public void setTagList(java.lang.String tagList)
        Tag list to be requested in the response message. All the tags are consecutive without any separator. If this field is missing, terminal will return the EMV data based on the default list.

        Default Tag List: 4F Application Identifier
        50 Application Label
        56 If tag 56 exists and is not encrypted, it shall be returned Track 1 Data
        57 If tag 57 is not encrypted, it shall be returned Track 2 Equivalent Data
        5A If tag 5A is not encrypted, it shall be returned Application Primary Account Number
        5F20 Cardholder Name
        5F24 Application Expiry Date
        5F25 Application Effective Date
        5F2D Language Preference
        5F30 Service Code
        5F34 Application Primary account Number sequence number
        9F11 Issuer Code Table Index
        9F12 Application preferred Name
        9F39 POS Entry Mode, indicates the method by which the PAN was entered, according to the first two digits of the ISO 8583 POS Entry Mode. As referring to ISO 8583 specification, the first 2 bytes of entry mode for ICC card is always “05”

        Parameters:
        tagList - Example: Request for tag 0x9F26 0x9F27 0x4F, then this field would be: “9F269F274F”

      • setPaddingChar

        public void setPaddingChar(java.lang.String paddingChar)
        This is used to indicate what value need to pad on data string before encryption.
        0: padding 0x00;
        F: padding 0xFF;
        The default padding char is 0. It is only valid when the Data Encryption Flag is not 0 (no encryption) or 4 (Voltage E2EE).

      • setTrackDataSentinel

        public void setTrackDataSentinel(java.lang.String trackDataSentinel)
        This is used to indicate the response track 1 and track 2 data whether need to include start and end sentinels for magnetic swipe transaction.
        0: needn’t to include start and end sentinel.
        1: need to include start and end sentinel.
        The default value is 1.

      • setKsnFlag

        public void setKsnFlag(java.lang.String ksnFlag)
        Indicate whether to increase KSN, when MAC key type is 1(DUKPT Key)

        Default KSN flag is 1(Increase)
        0: Not increase.
        1: Increase.

        Parameters:
        ksnFlag - Attribute: n1
        Since:
        V1.08.00

      • getReportStatus

        public java.lang.String getReportStatus()

      • setReportStatus

        public void setReportStatus(java.lang.String reportStatus)
        Whether to report status back.

        “1” to report.
        “0” not to report.
        Parameters:
        reportStatus - Attribute: n1
        Since:
        V1.08.00
